what_it_does: A preview card demonstrating the sidebar navigation icon convention: it renders a 12-item nav grid where each row uses a CV line icon (pulled from window.CV_ICONS.data by key) stroked in the bronze nav-glyph token colour, with the active row stroked in gold. It states the rule that the sidebar must use token-coloured CV line icons and never emoji.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation: group=Brand, name='Sidebar emoji convention', subtitle 'Bronze CV line icons as nav glyphs — never emoji', viewport='700x240'.
Loads ../assets/icons/cv-icons.js and reads CV_ICONS.data for the SVG path markup keyed by icon name.
Blurb states the rule: CV line icons stroked in --accent-bronze by default, active row in --accent-gold, never emoji, because glyphs must theme/recolour/stay on-brand.
ITEMS array maps 12 nav labels to icon keys (e.g. Projects→files-stack, Gallery→image marked active).
Active row styled with a 1.5px dashed --border-dashed border and gold-stroked glyph.
SVG glyphs are 17x17, fill:none, stroke via var(--accent-bronze), stroke-width 1.7, rounded caps/joins.
Falls back to empty string for any missing icon key (D[it[1]] || '').
standouts: Encodes a named brand law ('never emoji — use token-coloured CV line icons') as a rendered, demonstrable card.
Active state changes the stroke from a bronze to a gold token, showing the theming rationale for the rule in situ.
conventions: Icons sourced from a single registry (CV_ICONS.data) rather than inline SVG copies.
Stroke colours are token vars (--accent-bronze / --accent-gold), never literals — the very point of the rule.
notes: A brand-convention card asserting and demonstrating that the platform sidebar uses token-coloured CV line icons (bronze default, gold active) and never emoji, with glyphs pulled from the CV_ICONS registry.
questions: What is the full set of icon keys available in CV_ICONS.data, and do all 12 referenced keys resolve?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the card both states the never-emoji icon rule and demonstrates it by rendering CV line icons from the registry in token colours.
apparent_themes: Brand consistency / on-brand glyphs over emoji
intention_signals: Trying to codify and visibly justify the rule that navigation glyphs must be themeable token-coloured line icons (not emoji), so the sidebar recolours and stays on-brand under any theme.
novelty: Encoding an icon/brand convention as a live demonstration card that proves the themeability rationale by swapping stroke tokens for the active state.
contribution: Contributes the sidebar nav icon convention (token-coloured CV line icons, never emoji; bronze default / gold active) and a registry-driven glyph rendering pattern keyed by icon name.
summary_for_embedding: A claude-ds Brand preview card rendering a 12-item sidebar nav with CV line icons from the CV_ICONS registry, stroked in bronze (active row gold), asserting the never-emoji icon convention.
intention_for_embedding: To codify and demonstrate that sidebar navigation must use themeable token-coloured CV line icons rather than emoji so glyphs recolour and stay on-brand.
